ace: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Acehnese.
acre: noun. An English unit of land area (symbol: a. or ac.) originally denoting a day 's ploughing for a yoke of oxen, now standardized as 4,840
ale: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Aleut.
alec: noun. An anchovy or herring, especially pickled or dried.
alee: adverb. On the lee side of a ship, to the leeward side (vs aweather)
als: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tosk Albanian.
are: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Western Arrernte.
ars: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Najdi Arabic.
arse: noun. A person's buttocks; the bottom, the backside.
ass: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ipulo.
car: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Kari'na.
cares: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of care
caress: noun. An act of endearment; any act or expression of affection; an embracing, or touching, with tenderness.
carl: noun. A rude, rustic man; a churl.
carse: noun. Low, fertile land; a river valley.
case: noun. An actual event, situation, or fact.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cereal: noun. A type of grass (such as wheat, rice or oats) cultivated for its edible grains.
cess: noun. An assessed tax, duty, or levy; billeting.
class: noun. A group, collection, category or set sharing characteristics or attributes.
classer: noun. One who classes or classifies
clears: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of clear
crass: adjective. Coarse; crude; unrefined or insensitive; lacking discrimination or taste.
crease: noun. A line or mark made by folding or doubling any pliable substance; hence, a similar mark, however produced.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
cress: noun. A plant of various species, chiefly cruciferous.
ear: noun. The organ of hearing, consisting of the pinna or auricle, auditory canal, eardrum, malleus, incus, stapes and cochlea.
earl: noun. A British or Irish nobleman next in rank above a viscount and below a marquess; equivalent to a European count.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
era: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eravallan.
erases: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of erase
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.

lar: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Larteh.
lares: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Lares.
las: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gur Lama.
lase: verb. To use a laser beam on, as for cutting.
laser: noun. A device that produces a monochromatic, coherent beam of light.
lases: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lase
lass: noun. A girl; also, a young woman.
lea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lega-Shabunda.
lease: noun. An interest in land granting exclusive use or occupation of real estate for a limited period; a leasehold.
leaser: noun. One who leases or gleans; lessor.
leases: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of lease
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
leer: verb. To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.
lesser: adjective. Of two (or, rarely, more than two) things: the smaller in size (littler), in value, in importance etc.
race: noun. A contest between people, animals, vehicles, etc. where the goal is to be the first to reach some objective.
rale: noun. An abnormal clicking, rattling or crackling sound, made by one or both lungs and heard with a stethoscope, caused by the popping open
ras: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tegali.
rase: verb. To be extremely angry; to rage; specifically, of a dog or wolf: to snarl in rage.
real: adjective. True, genuine, not merely nominal or apparent.
recess: noun. A depressed, hollow, or indented space; also, a hole or opening.
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
resale: noun. Sale of something previously bought.
rescale: verb. To alter the scale of a drawing or project; to change the physical proportions.
rescales: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rescale
reseal: verb. To seal (something) again (in any sense of "apply a seal to").
reseals: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of reseal
sal: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Salishan languages.
sale: noun. An exchange of goods or services for currency or credit.
scale: noun. A ladder; a series of steps; a means of ascending.
scaler: noun. An electronic circuit that aggregates many signals into one.
scar: noun. A permanent mark on the skin, sometimes caused by the healing of a wound.
sea: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Semai.
seal: noun. A pinniped (Pinnipedia), particularly an earless seal (true seal) or eared seal.
sealer: noun. A tool used to seal something.
sear: adjective. Dry; withered, especially of vegetation.

see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.

ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
sera: noun. plural of serum; alternative form of serums
serac: noun. Often sérac: a hard, cone - shaped, pale green, strongly flavoured cheese from Switzerland made from skimmed cow milk and blue
seral: adjective. Of or pertaining to a sere.
